verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 is canonically mitochondrial but exhibits documented cell-surface accessibility under stress, inflammatory, and oncogenic conditions. 15-20% of total cellular HSPD1 localizes to extramitochondrial sites including the plasma membrane, particularly in non-baseline states. Surface presence increases during stress, inflammation, and tumorigenesis. In cancer cells, it accumulates at the PM and is secreted via exosomes. Stress-induced surface translocation and cancer ecto-display qualify as cell_state_induced contextual accessibility.
